data.title: TVB Limited Rebrands with New Corporate Name and Registered Address
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Television Broadcasts Limited ( (HK:0511) ) has shared an update.

TVB Limited has officially changed its English name from Television Broadcasts Limited to TVB Limited and its Chinese name from 電視廣播有限公司 to 無綫集團有限公司, effective 24 July 2026, following earlier shareholder approvals. Existing share certificates bearing the former name will remain valid for legal title, trading, settlement, registration and delivery, with new certificates issued only under the new name going forward.

The company emphasized that the name change does not affect the rights of shareholders or security holders, and it will later announce details of a new Chinese stock short name once confirmed by the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. TVB also updated its registered address to reflect the renaming of “TVB City” to “TVB,” while keeping the physical location of its registered office unchanged, signalling a brand consolidation without operational disruption.

More about Television Broadcasts Limited

TVB Limited, formerly known as Television Broadcasts Limited, is a Hong Kong-based media company primarily engaged in television broadcasting and related entertainment services. The company focuses on serving audiences in Hong Kong and broader Chinese-speaking markets, operating from its facilities in Tseung Kwan O Industrial Estate in the New Territories.

Average Trading Volume: 989,393

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$1.09B

data.title: Perenti trims potential equity overhang as 1.76m conditional rights lapse
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Perenti Global ( (AU:PRN) ) has provided an update.

Perenti Limited has notified the market that 1,764,508 conditional rights to securities, designated as PRNAC, have lapsed after the conditions attached to those rights were not satisfied by 20 July 2026. The cessation of these rights slightly reduces the company’s potential future issued capital, which may have a modest effect on prospective dilution for existing shareholders but does not change the current share count.

The announcement, lodged as an Appendix 3H with the ASX, formalises the removal of these conditional rights from Perenti’s capital structure. While the disclosure is largely administrative, it clarifies the company’s outstanding equity instruments and may be viewed as a minor cleanup of contingent obligations tied to performance or service conditions.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:PRN) stock is a Buy
with a A$2.70 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Perenti Global stock,
see the AU:PRN Stock Forecast page.

More about Perenti Global

Perenti Limited is an Australia-based mining services company listed on the ASX under the code PRN. The group typically provides contract mining, drilling and related support services to resource sector clients, focusing on large-scale projects in key mining jurisdictions.

Thrive Tribe Technologies Limited has reported that all resolutions put to shareholders at its extraordinary general meeting on 24 July 2026 were passed by poll, reflecting strong investor support. Voting results showed overwhelming majorities in favour of each resolution, indicating broad shareholder backing for the company’s capital-raising strategy and governance decisions. data.title: Green360 Lands First Commercial MKX Supply Deal with Holcim
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An announcement from Suvo Strategic Minerals ( (AU:GT3) ) is now available.

Green360 Technologies has secured its first binding commercial supply agreement with Holcim (Australia) for MKX‑CC, a proprietary low‑carbon calcined clay cement replacement product, marking Holcim as its inaugural commercial customer. The 12‑month agreement covers the supply of up to 4,800 tonnes of MKX into Holcim’s Victorian concrete operations and is expected to generate between $1 million and $2 million in revenue for Green360.

The deal provides independent commercial validation of MKX by one of the world’s largest construction materials companies following extensive technical evaluation, underscoring the product’s credibility in a key market. It also marks Green360’s shift from technology development to commercial execution and creates a platform for further supply agreements as the Australian concrete industry grapples with shortages of traditional cement substitutes, with potential benefits for shareholders through early revenue and market positioning in low‑carbon materials.

More about Suvo Strategic Minerals

Green360 Technologies, listed on the ASX as GT3, operates through its subsidiary Suvo Minerals Australia to develop and supply low‑carbon cement replacement products such as its proprietary high‑reactivity metakaolin MKX‑CC. The company targets the construction materials sector, aiming to address structural shortages of traditional supplementary cementitious materials in Australia’s concrete industry.

data.title: Desane Plans Orderly Chairmanship Transition as Long-Serving Chair Retires
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Desane Group Holdings Ltd ( (AU:DGH) ) just unveiled an update.

Desane Group Holdings Ltd has announced a planned leadership transition, with long-serving Chairman John Sheehan AM to retire as Chairman and Director at the conclusion of the company’s 2026 Annual General Meeting, expected in November. Sheehan, who has been a Director since 1987 and Chairman since 1992, will remain in his role until the AGM to ensure an orderly changeover.

Following its succession planning review, the Board intends to appoint existing Non-Executive Director Peter Krejci as Chairman immediately after Sheehan’s retirement, providing continuity of leadership and reinforcing the company’s governance framework. Both Sheehan and Krejci highlighted their commitment to executing Desane’s strategy and creating long-term shareholder value, with no immediate changes to the Board’s composition or committee structures anticipated.

More about Desane Group Holdings Ltd

Desane Group Holdings Ltd is a Sydney-based property investment and development company. It focuses on delivering long-term value for shareholders, underpinned by a culture of integrity, strong work ethic and a commitment to excellence in the Australian real estate market.

data.title: Aldoro boosts Kameelburg resource, claims world’s largest strontium deposit
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Aldoro Resources Ltd. ( (AU:ARN) ) has issued an update.

Aldoro Resources reported a significant upgrade to the Mineral Resource Estimate at its 85%-owned Kameelburg REE-Niobium-Strontium project in Namibia, confirming it as the world’s largest strontium resource and a globally strategic critical mineral asset outside dominant Asian supply chains. The updated JORC-compliant Inferred resource now stands at 597.07Mt at 2.49% TREO equivalent, with high-grade material doubling to 312.65Mt at 2.87% TREO equivalent, reflecting continued scale and grade growth over the past year.

Drilling during the quarter delivered record-length continuous high-grade intercepts and confirmed the company’s geological zoning model, showing high-grade niobium mineralisation on the margins and rare earths concentrated toward the core of the Kameelburg carbonatite. Aldoro also declared a maiden strontium resource of 596.01Mt at 2.17% Sr and reported hydrometallurgical tests achieving over 70% rare earth and nearly 100% strontium recoveries, while bolstering its balance sheet with an underwritten options deal and post-quarter convertible notes to support ongoing project advancement.

More about Aldoro Resources Ltd.

Aldoro Resources Ltd is an Australian-listed mineral exploration company focused on rare earth elements, niobium and strontium, with its flagship Kameelburg carbonatite project in central northern Namibia. The company is positioning itself as a supplier of critical minerals, targeting markets seeking diversification from China- and Iran-dominated supply chains.

Average Trading Volume: 162,918

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$66.28M

Odyssey Gold Limited has reported a change in the interests of director Levi Mochkin, who increased his indirect holdings in the company through on-market purchases between 17 and 23 July 2026. The increase in Mochkin’s shareholding may be interpreted as a vote of confidence in Odyssey Gold’s prospects, potentially reassuring investors about management’s alignment with shareholder interests. Key Petroleum Limited ( (AU:KEY) ) has provided an announcement.

Key Petroleum Limited spent the June quarter advancing its Potential Commercial Area applications over ATP 920 and ATP 924 in Queensland, maintaining close engagement with government authorities. Management views approval as a critical step to secure long-term tenure on core assets, underpin future exploration and development, and increase strategic flexibility in the Cooper Eromanga Basin.

In the coming quarter, Key Petroleum plans to hold its AGM, continue pursuing funding options, assess new petroleum acreage, and evaluate complementary business opportunities while keeping a disciplined approach to capital and risk.

data.title: Genomma Lab Earnings Call Balances Pressure and Recovery
data.description:
Genomma Lab Internacional SAB de CV Class B ((MX:LABB)) has held its Q2 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Genomma Lab Internacional’s latest earnings call struck a tone of cautious optimism as management balanced clear signs of operational recovery against persistent top-line pressure. Executives pointed to improving Mexican sellout, stronger margins and a solid financial position, while acknowledging currency headwinds, weaker U.S. demand and lower free cash flow as near-term challenges.

Sequential Recovery in Mexican Sellout

Mexican sellout trends showed a sharp sequential rebound, easing from a 15.7% decline in April to a 6.8% drop in June and turning positive at 2.4% in early July. The leading retailer moved from a 7% contraction in April to double-digit growth by July, and the sell-in to sell-out gap narrowed to just 43 basis points, signaling healthier inventory levels.

Suerox Momentum and Margin Expansion

Hydration brand Suerox remained a standout, delivering sustained double-digit growth at the leading retailer and pushing its share there to 12.2% in mid-July. Despite cutting prices and absorbing a one-peso-per-bottle tax, Suerox’s Mexico gross margin jumped 11.7 percentage points in the quarter and now sits 2.8 points above pre-discount levels.

Productivity Gains Lift Gross Margin

Company-wide productivity initiatives helped expand consolidated gross margin by 106 basis points to 64.6% in the second quarter. These structural gains were strong enough to offset higher promotional spending and the impact of the new tax, indicating that efficiency improvements are beginning to show through the P&L.

Steady Profitability and Net Income Growth

EBITDA reached MXN 959 million with a margin of 21.8%, 200 basis points lower year-on-year due to operational deleverage from weaker volumes. Even so, net income rose 5.5% to MXN 375 million, and the net margin improved by 93 basis points to 8.5%, helped by lower financial expenses and reduced foreign-exchange losses.

Regional Strength Outside Mexico

Latin America ex-Argentina, which now represents about 30% of the company’s mix, posted 5.6% growth led by Central America and the Andean region. Argentina grew 37.7%, outpacing inflation by 4.5 points, and Latin America like-for-like sales rose 3.9% as the region’s EBITDA margin edged up 41 basis points to 25.1%.

Solid Balance Sheet and Capital Actions

Trailing 12-month free cash flow stood at MXN 1.259 billion and leverage remained conservative, with net debt at 1.38 times EBITDA and debt service coverage at 5.2 times. The company paid its 16th consecutive quarterly dividend and, after quarter-end, secured a MXN 1.5 billion 10-year amortizing term loan to reinforce liquidity and extend its maturity profile.

Sales Decline and Currency Translation Hit

Reported net sales fell 6% year-on-year to MXN 4.397 billion, with like-for-like sales down 3.6%, reflecting weaker demand and strategic channel adjustments. Results were further dampened by a 10.8% appreciation of the Mexican peso, which created a sizable translation headwind on foreign operations.

U.S. Weakness and Hispanic Retail Disruption

The United States business remained under pressure as Hispanic retail channels faced disruption and cough-and-cold categories softened after milder seasons. Management cited declines that were more pronounced in local currency, prompting a realignment of commercial strategies and distribution to stabilize the region.

Operational Deleverage Weighs on EBITDA Margin

Lower volumes drove operational deleverage, compressing the EBITDA margin by 200 basis points to 21.8% despite flat SG&A and productivity gains. Management warned that this margin pressure is likely to persist for the remainder of 2026, with improvement tied to a gradual recovery in sales volumes.

Working Capital Build Pressures Free Cash Flow

Trailing 12-month free cash flow fell 53% versus the prior year as lower operating income combined with heavier working capital needs. The cash conversion cycle extended to 129 days, up 10 days from the prior quarter, reflecting deliberate increases in receivables and inventory to support new product launches.

Broad Category Contraction in Mexico

Mexico’s consumer health and personal care market remained challenging, with isotonic beverages down 6.6%, OTC categories off 6.3%, personal care slipping 1.4% and infant nutrition declining 1.1%. These broad-based contractions weighed on sellout, compounding the impact of company-specific channel decisions.

Inventory Risk from Seasonal Loading

Management acknowledged that heavy channel loading ahead of the World Cup and summer left trade inventories elevated, especially in isotonic beverages. With category demand falling short of expectations, the company faces the risk of future channel adjustments as distributors and retailers normalize stock levels.

CapEx and Short-Term Investment Drag

Capital expenditures reached MXN 120 million, including MXN 102 million earmarked for a plant and distribution center, underscoring ongoing investment in infrastructure. Additional working capital deployed to fund innovation and launches added to near-term cash flow pressure but is framed as necessary to support future growth.

Forward Guidance and Outlook

Management reiterated guidance for a gradual recovery through the second half of 2026, consistent with earlier warnings of a three-to-six-month window of EBITDA pressure. They expect operational deleverage to ease as sales recover, supported by better Mexican sellout, rising Suerox share and strong gross margins, while working capital should normalize as recent launches mature.

Genomma Lab’s call painted a picture of a business absorbing short-term hits to protect and grow market share while strengthening its financial foundations. For investors, the key question will be whether the early signs of recovery in Mexico and Latin America can offset U.S. weakness and category headwinds fast enough to validate management’s timeline for margin normalization.
